calendar.matters:
Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Suspension Driving Priviledge
Off Calendar May-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SUSPENSION DRIVING PRIVILEGE.
Appearances: Vanessa del Valle (certified law student) with Galit Lipa of Stanford Law School (650.723.1422) for petitioner and moving party; John T. McGlothlin of the Office of the Attorney General (510.622.2224) for respondent and opposing party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The weight of the evidence shows that Petitioner rebutted the three-hour presumption found in Vehicle Code section 23152(b). PAS tests are presumptively valid. (Manriquez v. Gourley (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 1227, 1232; Evidence Code section 664.) Respondent failed to produce evidence to show Petitioner's PAS results were invalid. Additionally, Respondent failed to produce evidence that shows Petitioner's BAC was over the legal limit while she was driving. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Suspension Driving Priviledge
Off Calendar May-14-2012 Continued To May-18-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Suspension of Driving Priviledge is Continued to May 18, 2012 per agreement of parties. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
